reply_content:
@Threefour_opennanoor Ahh; the standard 'led right to' and 'bruises' [here: also on the neck]. That is the product of writer Harold Schechter out of his '89 book. Those claims are 180-degrees off of the reports from the day of the event. There was no 'getting away with it' because there were no signs of any foul play (also- there was no autopsy). This account - tho obviously a 'juicy' addition to Gein lore - is a fabrication.

RE a scenario of a person 'laying on scorched ground but not being burned'- Henry obviously passed after the fire was extinguished. He had known health issues.
